text.title: AI Risk Aging Agent Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every AI Risk Aging Agent application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them. Plus a skip-methodology rule for decision-tree leaves that exit the methodology.

rule.statement: Apply to a single project's risk register. Cross-project aggregation requires a separate methodology.
Risk register rows carry id, statement, owner, severity, last_updated (ISO), dependent_tasks (int). Missing any = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Untyped registers are folklore; typed inputs make aging detectable.

rule.statement: Every risk has a named owner. No 'team' or 'we'. Anonymous risk = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Anonymous risks are how risks decay.

rule.statement: Risks not updated in 14 days are auto-flagged for review. Threshold is configurable but must be declared in the report.
rule.rationale: Stale risks are the dominant failure mode in distressed projects.

rule.statement: Every agent action (refresh suggestion, escalation) cites a specific input field (ticket id, owner non-response, dependent change). No ungrounded actions.
rule.rationale: Ungrounded agent suggestions waste owner attention.

rule.statement: Daily report carries ISO date + semver. Prior reports are append-only.
Skip this methodology when the decision tree resolves to a skip branch — preconditions are not met OR an adjacent methodology fits better.
rule.rationale: Skipping cleanly preserves the integrity of the rubric; misapplied methodologies produce false positives downstream.
